works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Salute to Eddie Condon
Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others
Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: people like Henry “Red” Allen, J.C. Higginbotham, Coleman Hawkins even, I mean they’ve been swept under the carpet, but they’ve pretty much been forgotten.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: I went to Europe with Sammy... it was Doc Cheetam and Sammy and J. C. Higginbotham... we took the boat over... We went to Paris

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: I remember going out on a gig with J. C. Higginbotham, and PeeWee Russell, and Buck Clayton

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: where I remember Don Byas and J.C. Higginbotham were in a small group there. And they would catch all — you know that was the days when there were more radio broadcasts than there were records. But they came on so late and my folks wouldn’t let me stay up to listen. But I’d ease up and crack the doo
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Fairconnetue
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee does not mention J.C. Higginbotham; the interviewer mentions him, and the interviewee responds with unrelated anecdotes about other musicians.

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: The incumbent band for a very long time was Red Allen... Red had this band with Buster Bailey, J. C. Higginbotham, and for a while, Cozy Cole on drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
